response: The internet began as a U.S. Defense Department project called ARPANET, which sent its first message in 1969. Key milestones—the TCP/IP protocols (1983), Tim Berners-Lee’s World Wide Web (1989), and the Mosaic browser (1993)—opened the network to the public, fueling the dot-com boom, the rise of social media, and mobile internet after the iPhone’s 2007 launch. Today, it connects over 5 billion people and underpins nearly all commerce, communication, and entertainment.
